result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: Perform a post-order traversal of the final tree. For each node u in the final tree, identify its children v1, v2, ..., vk. These children correspond to contiguous blocks of siblings in the original tree. For each block of original siblings that were merged into a single child vi in the final tree, perform the required k-1 merges between adjacent siblings in the original tree (from left to right) to form that child. JUSTIFICATION: A post-order traversal ensures that all merges within subtrees are completed before the merges at the current node's level are performed. Since merges only affect siblings, processing from the leaves upward allows us to reconstruct the exact sequence of operations needed to transform the original sibling sets into the final sibling sets.
